new_classes.label_en: Wine with PDO in containers > 2 L with actual alcoholic strength > 15% vol (CN 2204 29 28)
new_classes.label_de: Wein mit g.U. in Behältern > 2 L mit einem vorhandenen Alkoholgehalt > 15 % vol (KN 2204 29 28)
new_classes.definition_en: Still wine of fresh grapes bearing a Protected Designation of Origin (PDO) as defined in Additional Note 6(a) to Chapter 22, put up in containers holding more than 2 litres, with an actual alcoholic strength exceeding 15% vol.

coverage_explanation: The three key distinguishing criteria for CN 2204 29 28 are: (1) wine of fresh grapes bearing a PDO — captured via the PDOOriginQuality restriction; (2) presented in containers > 2 L — captured via the LargeContainer (≥ 2 L) part restriction using the existing TBox class (note: LargeContainer uses ≥ 2 L; the exact boundary > 2 L vs ≥ 2 L cannot be expressed more precisely without an exclusive lower-bound container class, hence slight under-coverage); (3) actual alcoholic strength > 15% vol — captured via the decimalRange restriction on alcoholByVolumePercent. The reference to Additional Note 6(a) to Chapter 22 (defining PDO for wines) is acknowledged but that note's content is not separately modelled here as it is already captured by the existing PDOOriginQuality class. Coverage is set to 0.85 due to the imprecision of LargeContainer (≥ 2 L rather than strictly > 2 L), which slightly overlaps with the 2 L boundary.
